TSA staffing shortages pose an existential threat to small airport operations, potentially forcing terminal closures and disrupting regional aviation accessibility.

DPE Class Action Targets FAA Termination Policies

Lawsuit alleges loss of appeal rights and procedural protections.

FAA's DPE termination procedures face legal challenge over inadequate appeal rights, potentially affecting examiner job security and certification processes.

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective safety risk management requires organizations to systematically select proportionate controls and formally document acceptance of residual risks that cannot be eliminated.

The Striking Differences Passengers Notice Between The Airbus A380 & Boeing 747

The A380's double-deck full-length cabin and 50% larger capacity fundamentally transform passenger experience economics compared to the 747's upper-deck configuration.

General Aviation Mar 19, 2026

FAA Aviation Safety at Oshkosh

Go behind the scenes at EAA AirVenture with the FAA's Aviation Safety organization. Meet the people who help ensure safety at this event and provide essential information resources to the general aviation community.

The FAA's presence at AirVenture demonstrates proactive community engagement and direct safety education to general aviation pilots at North America's largest aviation gathering.

Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space plan debris removal service

Portal Space Systems, a company developing maneuverable spacecraft, is partnering with an Australian startup to offer a commercial orbital debris removal service. The post Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space plan debris removal service appeared first on SpaceNews.

Commercial debris removal services are emerging as critical infrastructure for sustainable orbital operations and future space commerce.

Commercial Mar 19, 2026

Press Release: British Airways’ first Starlink flight, a fitted 787, takes off

British Airways’ first aircraft equipped with Starlink Wi-Fi took to the skies today, the first flight operated by a UK airline featuring the innovative technology. The 787-8 aircraft bound for Houston is the first in the airline’s fleet to be connected, giving customers free access to fast, reli...

British Airways' Starlink-equipped 787 deployment signals a competitive shift toward satellite connectivity as a standard passenger amenity in long-haul operations.

See Runway Girl Network →

Pilot reported in stable condition following incident under investigation.

F-35 emergency landing over Iran highlights operational vulnerabilities and investigation importance for military aviation safety protocols.

Military Mar 19, 2026

U.S. F-35 Makes Emergency Landing After Allegedly Being Hit by Iranian Fire

CENTCOM says a U.S. F-35 made an emergency landing at a U.S. air base in the Middle East after it “was struck by what is believed to be Iranian fire,” according to sources quoted by CNN. A U.S. F-35 Lightning II fighter jet was forced to make an emergency landing at a U.S. air base […]

F-35 combat resilience demonstrated through successful emergency landing after hostile engagement, validating aircraft survivability in contested airspace.

See The Aviationist →

Industry Mar 19, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across airlines, airports, and flight operations is critical to handle rising air traffic while meeting environmental sustainability demands.

See MDPI →

EgyptAir's A350-900 deployment of Panasonic's IFEC systems demonstrates continued industry investment in premium passenger connectivity and engagement technologies.

Collins' pathway to Space Shuttle command—rigorous military aviation training, test pilot experience, and demonstrated leadership—establishes the foundational competencies required for autonomous aerospace vehicle operations.

Press Release: Tickets on sale for United’s 787-9 with Elevated interior

Starting today, travelers can book seats on United’s most premium international aircraft yet — the 787-9 Dreamliner with the United Elevated interior. Unveiled last May, this new aircraft has been redesigned from nose-to-tail with 99 total premium seats — the highest of percentage among U.S. carr...

United's 787-9 with elevated interior maximizes premium seating density at 99 seats, positioning the airline competitively in high-margin international markets.

Wildlife conservation at launch facilities demonstrates aerospace operations' compatibility with environmental stewardship and ecosystem protection.

How NASA is Collecting Explosion Data for Next Generation Rockets

Commercial launch providers continue to advance propulsion technology with a renewed focus on liquid oxygen and methane propelled rockets and spacecraft. As systems grow in scale, carrying millions of pounds of propellant, so too does the responsibility to fully understand the safety profile. Eng...

NASA's explosion data collection on scaled-up LOX/methane systems is critical for validating safety protocols as commercial launchers push toward heavier lift capabilities.

Aerospace Mar 19, 2026

NASA Laser Reflecting Instrument Makes GPS Satellite More Accurate

A NASA laser reflecting technology that will aid Global Positioning System (GPS) accuracy is now operational as of March 9. The instrument, known as a laser retroreflector array, or LRA, launched aboard GPS III SV-09, the ninth of U.S. Space Force’s Block III Global Positioning System satellites,...

NASA's laser retroreflector technology on GPS III SV-09 enhances positioning accuracy, improving navigation precision for aviation operations globally.

See NASA →

European airlines are pushing for mandatory 21-day strike notice requirements on air traffic controllers to mitigate operational disruptions and improve schedule predictability.

March action follows January temporary exemptions tied to broader foreign UAS restrictions.

Military Mar 19, 2026

A-10s Now Hunting Iranian Fast Attack Craft in the Strait of Hormuz

U.S. Air Force A-10s in maritime attack role are hunting small Iranian fast attack boats that could threaten shipping or naval forces in the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. Air Force A-10 Thundebolt II jets supporting operation Epic Fury have started flying a different type of mission. As explained by...

A-10 Warthogs are adapting to maritime patrol missions in the Persian Gulf, demonstrating aircraft flexibility beyond traditional close air support roles.
